sentence_reviews.text: LLM-as-judge uses a model to score, compare, critique, or classify outputs under a rubric, often calibrated against human labels and bias checks.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Gu et al. 2024 survey abstract and scope: LLMs used as evaluators, reliability, consistency, bias mitigation, benchmarks, and human/expert evaluation comparison; NIST AI RMF evaluation and risk-management framing.
sentence_reviews.notes: Narrowed to judge scoring, critique, calibration, and bias/instability limitations supported by LLM-as-judge survey framing.

sentence_reviews.text: LLM-as-judge methods can scale rubric-based scoring and critique, but they still need calibration against human judgments and checks for bias or instability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Gu et al. 2024 survey abstract and scope: LLMs used as evaluators, reliability, consistency, bias mitigation, benchmarks, and human/expert evaluation comparison; NIST AI RMF evaluation and risk-management framing.
sentence_reviews.notes: Narrowed to judge scoring, critique, calibration, and bias/instability limitations supported by LLM-as-judge survey framing.

sentence_reviews.text: Judge models can inherit model biases, favor familiar style over correctness, and disagree with humans when prompts, rubrics, or domains shift.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Gu et al. 2024 survey abstract and scope: LLMs used as evaluators, reliability, consistency, bias mitigation, benchmarks, and human/expert evaluation comparison; NIST AI RMF evaluation and risk-management framing.
sentence_reviews.notes: Narrowed to judge scoring, critique, calibration, and bias/instability limitations supported by LLM-as-judge survey framing.

sentence_reviews.text: LLM-as-judge methods are commonly calibrated, validated, or compared against human evaluation and preference studies.

sentence_reviews.text: LLM-as-judge evaluations commonly use benchmark tasks, rubrics, or task metrics to structure judge prompts and compare outputs.
